text: The surgeon uses an optical trocar technique to enter the abdominal cavity, establishing pneumoperitoneum. A laparoscopic transversus abdominis plane block is placed, followed by the insertion of pre-planned trocars along a bikini line incision. Robotic instruments are inserted, and the gallbladder is retracted cephalad.

The surgeon encounters adhesions between the omentum and gallbladder, which are carefully dissected using monopolar hook cautery while monitoring the duodenum's position to avoid injury. As the dissection approaches the duodenum, the gallbladder is retracted cephalad and then grasped in a caudal direction. The peritoneum of the gallbladder is incised with the hook cautery, creating a plane between the gallbladder and liver.

The surgeon retracts the gallbladder cephalad and proceeds with dissection of the closed triangle using hook cautery in a lateral to medial orientation. Indocyanine green fluorescence imaging is utilized to visualize the gallbladder, ensuring a bloodless plane between the gallbladder and liver bed. Adhesions to the neck of the gallbladder are carefully dissected, maintaining caution near the duodenum. The cystic artery and duct are identified using ICG fluorescence, and a window is created between them for precise dissection. The gallbladder is dissected away from the liver in the medial plane, achieving the critical view of safety.

The surgeon clips the cystic artery twice and the cystic duct three times, with an additional clip placed on the gallbladder side, before transecting the cystic duct. The cystic artery is then transected using electrocautery. The gallbladder is carefully removed from the liver using hook electrocautery, maintaining a bloodless avascular plane between the gallbladder and liver without violating either structure.

The surgeon inserts an endocatch bag percutaneously and deposits the completely removed gallbladder into it. The gallbladder is extracted intact from the abdomen without any spillage of biliary contents. The extraction site is closed using a transvascular suture passer in a figure-of-eight fashion, resulting in a cosmetically desirable outcome.
